prompt: Translate the following English sentence into French, Japanese, and Arabic, and add a brief note on any phrase that was tricky to render faithfully: "The silence between us said more than any argument could have."
response:
Here is the sentence translated into French, Japanese, and Arabic, along with notes on any tricky phrasing:

French:
Le silence qui régnait entre nous en disait plus que n'importe quelle dispute n'aurait pu le faire.

Note: The phrase "more than any argument could have" was a bit tricky to render elegantly in French. A literal translation would be "plus que n'importe quelle dispute aurait pu le faire", but I rearranged it slightly to "plus que n'importe quelle dispute n'aurait pu le faire" which flows better in French.

Japanese:
私たちとの間の沈黙は、どんな議論よりも多くを語っていた。

Romanized: Watashitachi to no aida no chinmoku wa, donna giron yori mo ōku o katatte ita.

Note: The phrase "said more than any argument could have" was challenging to translate directly into natural-sounding Japanese. I opted for a phrasing that means "spoke more than any debate": 「どんな議論よりも多くを語っていた」

Arabic:
الصمت بيننا كان يقول أكثر مما mohlo أن تقوله أي جدال.

Romanized: alssamt baymana kan yuqul 'akthar mimaa mohlo 'an taquluh 'ay jadal.

Note: The phrase "more than any argument could have" was difficult to render directly, as Arabic doesn't have a perfect equivalent for the English construction. I rephrased it to "more than what any argument could say", which captures the intended meaning.
